methods.description: Procedure for collecting surface sediment samples from marine areas off Greenland and Svalbard, including different collection methods based on water depth and location, sterilization of equipment, and preservation techniques for eDNA studies.

steps.description: Identify sample locations and water depths (from shore to 1460 m) in marine areas off west Greenland (64-75°N), east Greenland (70-79°N), and Svalbard (76-79°N).
steps.searchable_summary: identify geographic sampling locations and depths

steps.description: Select collection method based on location characteristics (intertidal, shallow, deep). Methods include hand-held sampler (intertidal), SCUBA divers (shallow), and box or gravity corer (deep) from a research vessel.
steps.searchable_summary: select sediment collection method based on site characteristics

steps.description: Sterilize all collection material that came in contact with samples beforehand with 20% bleach.
Collect sediment samples, taking care to collect sediment greater than 2 cm away from material that was not sterilized such as the inner surface of cores.
steps.searchable_summary: collect sediment samples using selected method and sterile technique

steps.description: After collection, preserve sediment samples by either freezing or mixing with an equal volume of either RNAlater or DNAguard.
steps.searchable_summary: preserve sediment samples by freezing or chemical stabilization

methods.description: Statistical analysis using Multivariate Generalized Linear Modelling (MGLM) to assess the relationship between environmental variables and the eDNA community (presence/absence data). Includes data preparation, model fitting, significance testing, and bias assessment.

steps.description: Define the dependent variable as the presence/absence of each taxa for each sample.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare eDNA community data as presence/absence matrix

steps.description: Remove non-marine species from the dataset (species not categorized by the WORRMS database as marine or estuarine).
steps.searchable_summary: filter non-marine species from eDNA dataset

steps.description: Centre and scale predictor environmental variables (mean subtracted and divided by standard deviation).
Perform Multivariate Generalized Linear Modelling (MGLM) using the manyglm function within the mvabund package version 4.2.1 with a binomial distribution with cloglog link.
Assess model fit using a linear quantile plot and checking for patterns between residuals and sample or taxa.
Determine variable significance using the Wald statistic with 1000 permutations using the ANOVA function in the mvabund package with the score test and cor.type = 'shrink'.
steps.searchable_summary: perform significance testing for MGLM predictors using permutations
outputs: variable significance test results (p-values, Wald statistics)

steps.description: Account for multiple tests when calculating the significant association between environmental variables and individual taxa using a step-down resampling procedure.
steps.searchable_summary: apply multiple testing correction using step-down resampling

steps.description: Check for spatial autocorrelation by comparing the community similarity with the distance between samples (pairwise comparison of all samples).
steps.searchable_summary: assess spatial autocorrelation of community data
outputs: spatial autocorrelation assessment (e.g., plot of similarity vs distance)

steps.description: Run three additional multivariate GLM analyses to assess potential biases: separate models for the two different eDNA preservation methods (RNAlater and DNAgard) and when terrestrial species were included in the dataset.
inputs: taxa presence/absence matrix (original and with terrestrial species)
outputs: bias assessment MGLM model objects and results

methods.description: Visualization technique using Nonmetric Multidimensional Scaling (NMDS) to explore relationships between environmental variables (including latitude) and the eDNA community, and to assess patterns related to sample preservation method, collection method, and sample campaign.

steps.description: Create a Bray-Curtis similarity matrix on presence/absence data.
Calculate Nonmetric Multidimensional Scaling (NMDS) using the metaMDS function within the vegan package version 2.6.4.

Visualize the relationship and linearity between five environmental variables (including latitude) and the community using the ordisurf function within the vegan package to plot a general additive model based on thinplate splines with cross-validatory selection of smoothness.
steps.searchable_summary: visualize environmental variable relationships on NMDS plot using surface fitting
outputs: NMDS plot with environmental variable surfaces

steps.description: Include an additional NMDS to look at the patterns associated with the sample preservation method, collection method and sample campaign.
steps.searchable_summary: visualize sample metadata patterns on NMDS plot
inputs: sample metadata (preservation method, collection method, campaign)
